name: Phil Taylor
highlights: 16x World Champion ‚ÄĒ an all-time record
11 perfect legs in televised play ‚ÄĒ more than anyone else
72-match unbeaten run in 2001
First player to average over 100 in a televised match
6 consecutive World Championship titles (1995‚Äď2002)
quote: I just love darts. I eat, sleep and breathe it.
fun_fact: Taylor was handed his first set of darts by Eric Bristow, who also personally sponsored his early career ‚ÄĒ without that moment, darts history looks very different.
bio: Widely regarded as the greatest darts player of all time, Phil Taylor dominated the sport for over three decades. His 16 World Championship titles are a record that may never be broken.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: Phil Taylor ‚ÄĒ 16x World Champion and the greatest darts player who ever lived. #DartsDaily

name: Michael van Gerwen
walk_on_music: Seven Nation Army ‚ÄĒ The White Stripes
highlights: 3x PDC World Champion (2014, 2017, 2019)
Highest ever televised average: 123.40
9 televised nine-dart finishes
Record 7 Premier League Darts titles
Youngest ever PDC ranking event winner at age 16
quote: I'm not arrogant. I'm confident. There's a difference.
fun_fact: Van Gerwen won his first PDC ranking event aged just 16 ‚ÄĒ making him the youngest winner in PDC history. He was still in school at the time.
bio: The most dominant player of the modern era, Michael van Gerwen has redefined what is possible in darts. His scoring power and finishing ability are unmatched, and he continues to set records that were once thought impossible.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: Michael van Gerwen ‚ÄĒ 3x World Champion and the most feared player on tour. #DartsDaily

name: Raymond van Barneveld
highlights: 5x World Champion (4x BDO, 1x PDC)
Defeated Phil Taylor 7‚Äď6 in the greatest World Championship final ever played (2007)
First Dutch player to win a World Championship
Grand Prix and UK Open winner multiple times
Came out of retirement in 2022 to rejoin the PDC tour
quote: When I beat Taylor that night, I felt like I was walking on air.
fun_fact: Before darts, Barneveld worked as a postman in The Hague. He used to practice darts in his lunch breaks and after his deliveries.
bio: One of the most beloved figures in darts history, Raymond van Barneveld won five World Championships across two tours. His legendary 7‚Äď6 victory over Phil Taylor in 2007 is widely considered the greatest match ever played.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: Raymond van Barneveld ‚ÄĒ Barney! 5x World Champion and hero of the greatest darts final ever. #DartsDaily

name: Luke Littler
highlights: PDC World Champion 2025 ‚ÄĒ aged just 17
Reached the 2024 World Championship final aged 16
Became the youngest player to win a PDC ranking event
Won the 2024 World Series of Darts Finals
Averaged 130+ in multiple televised matches
quote: I just play my game. I don't really think about the pressure.
fun_fact: Littler's favourite food is pizza ‚ÄĒ a fact that became a running joke and viral moment during his 2024 World Championship run, spawning countless memes.
bio: The teenage sensation who took the darts world by storm. Luke Littler burst onto the scene at the 2024 PDC World Championship as a 16-year-old, reaching the final and capturing the hearts of millions before going on to win the title a year later.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: Luke Littler ‚ÄĒ The Nuke. World Champion at 17 and the future of darts. #DartsDaily

name: Fallon Sherrock
highlights: First woman ever to beat a man at the PDC World Championship (2019)
Beat two men at the 2019 Worlds ‚ÄĒ back to back
Multiple Women's Series event winner
Sparked a global surge in women taking up darts
Featured in Time magazine's 100 most influential people
quote: I just wanted to show that women belong on that stage.
fun_fact: Fallon became a mother at 16 and used darts as a creative outlet and escape. She went on to make history on the sport's biggest stage just a few years later.
bio: Fallon Sherrock rewrote the history books at the 2019 PDC World Championship, becoming the first woman ever to beat a man at the tournament. Her courage and composure under the Alexandra Palace lights inspired millions and opened doors for a new generation.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: Fallon Sherrock ‚ÄĒ The Queen of the Palace and the woman who made darts history. #DartsDaily

name: Gary Anderson
highlights: 2x PDC World Champion (2015, 2016)
Back-to-back World titles in consecutive years
Premier League Darts winner multiple times
Known for one of the most unorthodox throwing styles in the game
Struggled with back and shoulder injuries throughout career
quote: I never think I'm going to lose. I always believe I can win.
fun_fact: Anderson's throwing style is famously unorthodox ‚ÄĒ his elbow drops as he throws, something coaches would tell beginners never to do. Yet it produced two World titles.
bio: Gary Anderson is one of the most naturally talented players ever to pick up a dart. His relaxed demeanour and unconventional technique belie a fierce competitor who has claimed two World Championship titles and numerous ranking event victories.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: Gary Anderson ‚ÄĒ The Flying Scotsman. 2x World Champion with one of the most unique throwing styles in darts. #DartsDaily

name: Adrian Lewis
highlights: 2x PDC World Champion (2011, 2012)
Won back-to-back World titles aged 25 and 26
Prot√©g√© and close friend of Phil Taylor
Multiple ranking event winner on PDC tour
One of Stoke-on-Trent's most celebrated sporting exports
quote: When you walk out at Alexandra Palace, nothing else matters.
fun_fact: Lewis is a lifelong close friend of Phil Taylor ‚ÄĒ both are from Stoke-on-Trent, and Taylor was a major influence on Lewis's development as a player from a young age.
bio: Adrian Lewis burst onto the scene with back-to-back PDC World Championship titles in 2011 and 2012. Nicknamed Jackpot for his explosive winning ability, he remains one of the most talented players of his generation.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: Adrian Lewis ‚ÄĒ Jackpot! 2x World Champion from Stoke and one of darts' most explosive finishers. #DartsDaily

name: Eric Bristow
highlights: Dominated darts throughout the 1980s
Helped launch the career of Phil Taylor
One of the founding fathers of modern professional darts
MBE awarded for services to darts in 1989
quote: I was the best. Simple as that.
fun_fact: Bristow personally sponsored a young Phil Taylor's entry into professional darts ‚ÄĒ effectively creating his own greatest ever rival and successor.
bio: Eric Bristow was the defining figure of darts in the 1980s. The Crafty Cockney won five World Championship titles and was the sport's biggest personality for over a decade, helping to bring darts into the mainstream on British television.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: Eric Bristow ‚ÄĒ The Crafty Cockney. 5x World Champion and one of darts' all-time greats. #DartsDaily

name: John Lowe
highlights: 3x World Champion (1979, 1987, 1993)
Hit the first ever televised nine-dart finish (1984)
Won ¬£102,000 for that perfect leg ‚ÄĒ record at the time
Competed professionally across five decades
One of the true founding fathers of televised darts
quote: That nine-darter? I knew what I was doing every single dart.
fun_fact: When John Lowe hit the first televised nine-dart finish in 1984, he won ¬£102,000 in prize money ‚ÄĒ roughly equivalent to ¬£320,000 in today's money. He reportedly stayed completely calm throughout.
bio: John Lowe is a true legend of darts who competed at the highest level for nearly three decades. Best known for throwing the first ever televised nine-dart finish in 1984, Old Stoneface won three World Championship titles and helped shape the modern game.
share_text: ūüéĮ DartsDaily Player of the Day: John Lowe ‚ÄĒ Old Stoneface. 3x World Champion and the man who threw the first ever televised nine-dart finish. #DartsDaily

name: Peter Wright
highlights: 2x PDC World Champion (2020, 2022)
Known for the most colourful hair and outfits in darts history
Multiple ranking event winner across his career
Didn't win his first major until his 40s ‚ÄĒ a late bloomer story
Grand Prix and Masters champion
quote: The hair, the outfit ‚ÄĒ it's all part of who I am. I want people to enjoy the show.
fun_fact: Wright once arrived at a tournament with his hair dyed to look like the Scottish flag. His wife Joanne designs many of his outfits ‚ÄĒ darts as a couple's project.
bio: Peter Wright is one of the most colourful characters in darts ‚ÄĒ literally. The two-time World Champion is as famous for his wild hair and extravagant outfits as he is for his dart throwing, but behind the showmanship is one of the most complete players on tour.
